title: rubus lingulatus
intro: Rubus lingulatus, also known as the fringed raspberry or fringed bramble, is a species of raspberry native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves, which contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus lingulatus include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Rubus lingulatus is typically prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
